raw_consumer_statement: While trying to track an order I placed on the XXXX app, I called a number I found on XXXX for their customer service team. A person named " XXXX XXXX '' answered, I asked if this was the XXXX Customer Care and he said yes and that I had used the wrong spelling on my email address to place my order. He asked me to download an app called XXXX, said he would monitor my screen and asked me to send an email with my area code via my XXXX account. I was suspicious cause it seemed to me like transferring money ( I had never used a XXXX Gift card before ). I sent an email with my area code but my credit card bank notified me that I had transferred {$300.00}. I immediately called him out and he said it was because my XXXX  email id did not match the email id I used to place the order. He said if I transfer {$190.00} they would send me a gift card worth the total amount of {$500.00}. I was extremely suspicious and also desperate at this point because I lost {$300.00}. After making the XXXX  transfer, he told me to get a XXXX gift card to which they would transfer the money. I had no idea scammers used gift cards to scam so I proceeded to do exactly that. I got the git card which he made me take a picture of. All the while, the XXXX app was on XXXX mode on my phone, which he said was " only connecting to the server ''. I did not use any passwords because I have enabled Face ID but they did get me to try and use XXXX as a payment method for which I needed my SSN. XXXX did not verify my identity immediately so I dont believe my SSN is compromised. After I purchased the gift card and they failed to refund my money immediately, I was really suspicious and turned off XXXX on the XXXX app and gave an ultimatum that I would report them to the authorities if I don't receive my refund as soon as possible. He said it would take a couple hours for the money to be reflected on my account. I waited for over an hour and decided to call XXXX, explain the situation and cancel the gift card. XXXX obliged immediately. I called my credit card company ( XXXX Citi XXXX XXXX and explained the situation and asked them to try and cancel the pending transactions. They could not cancel but they disputed the transactions once posted. I was able to recover the {$500.00} from the XXXX gift card but am awaiting the result of the investigation from my bank on the {$500.00} transferred via XXXX Gift Card. XXXX said they could not help as it was a digital good and had already been used. Citi is denying my claim and will not reimburse me.
